24.reason: [If the file "article.txt" does not exist or cannot be opened, fp will be NULL, and subsequent fgetc(fp) leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [If the file "article.txt" does not exist or cannot be opened, fp will be NULL, and subsequent fgetc(fp) leads to undefined behavior.]

29.reason: [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When EOF (typically -1) is stored in a char, it may become positive due to signed/unsigned char ambiguity, causing infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When EOF (typically -1) is stored in a char, it may become positive due to signed/unsigned char ambiguity, causing infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]

30.reason: [tolower expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Passing a char that may be negative (if plain char is signed) leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [tolower expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Passing a char that may be negative (if plain char is signed) leads to undefined behavior.]

32.reason: [isalpha also requires input as unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Same issue as above with negative char values.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.2 , reason:  [isalpha also requires input as unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Same issue as above with negative char values.]

35.reason: [Same type issue: c is char, but fgetc returns int. Also, if n exceeds 19, buffer overflow occurs since w[20] can hold max 19 letters + '\0'.]
